name: autoscale
description: a compute autoscaling world reacting to load; actions scale_up, scale_down, hold

card.description: a compute autoscaling world reacting to load; actions scale_up, scale_down, hold

rules: 'scale_up' adds one instance, never exceeding max_servers.
'scale_down' removes one instance, never dropping below min_servers.
Each step, demand (load) advances along a deterministic curve unless a perceived reading overrides it before the step.
total_capacity = servers * capacity_per_server; utilization = load / total_capacity.
Requests beyond total_capacity are dropped (dropped = max(0, load - total_capacity)).
cost accrues by servers * cost_per_server every step.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name", "hold") if isinstance(action, dict) else str(action)

    lo = int(s.get("min_servers", 1))
    hi = int(s.get("max_servers", 20))
    servers = int(s.get("servers", 1))

    # 1) apply the autoscaling decision, clamped to the group bounds
    if name == "scale_up":
        servers = min(hi, servers + 1)
    elif name == "scale_down":
        servers = max(lo, servers - 1)
    # "hold" (or anything unknown) leaves the fleet unchanged
    s["servers"] = servers

    # 2) advance the exogenous demand on a deterministic curve (a daily-style
    #    swell). Live perception can override "load" before the next step.
    t = int(s.get("t", 0))
    base = float(s.get("load_base", 600.0))
    amp = float(s.get("load_amp", 350.0))
    load = int(round(base + amp * math.sin(t / 2.5)))
    load = max(0, load)
    s["load"] = load

    # 3) recompute health against the (possibly resized) fleet
    cap_each = int(s.get("capacity_per_server", 100))
    total_capacity = servers * cap_each
    s["total_capacity"] = total_capacity
    if total_capacity > 0:
        s["utilization"] = round(load / total_capacity, 3)
    else:
        s["utilization"] = 0.0
    s["dropped"] = max(0, load - total_capacity)

    # 4) accrue cost for running the fleet this step
    cost_each = float(s.get("cost_per_server", 1.0))
    s["cost"] = round(float(s.get("cost", 0.0)) + servers * cost_each, 3)

    s["t"] = t + 1
    return s

emit.report: Fleet: {servers} instances serving load={load} (utilization {utilization}); dropped={dropped}; cost={cost}.
